Kelun-Merck validate TROP2-Keytruda pairing in lung cancer with improved survival
snippet: Treatment with the TROP2 ADC sac-TMT led to a 70% objective response rate and progression-free survival was “significantly improved” as compared to placebo—the second positive readout for the asset this week.

title: Recordati fields $12.4B go-private offer
snippet: Going private could give Recordati strategic flexibility and a stable source of capital, according to CVC Capital Partners and Groupe Bruxelles Lambert, which are offering to take the Italian pharma private for a 13% premium.

title: Gilead inks $140M API supply deal, tightening ties to Korean manufacturer
snippet: The agreement is the largest in a series of deals that Gilead Sciences has penned with Korea’s Yuhan.

title: AbbVie, Novartis slash US headcount
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: Biogen, Denali pull plug on Parkinson’s program after mid-stage flop
snippet: Biogen and Denali’s Parkinson’s disease drug failed to significantly slow disease progression in a Phase 2b study, missing both primary and secondary endpoints.

title: Moderna’s once-rebuffed mRNA flu shot to face scrutiny from FDA adcomm
snippet: After getting slapped with a surprise refuse-to-file letter signed by former CBER Director Vinay Prasad, Moderna’s flu vaccine application will now go before the FDA’s Vaccine advisory committee.

title: How To Shine As Biotech Comes Back From the Brink
snippet: Biotech has been pulling off some remarkable science of late, while M&A and IPOs return to bolster the sector. But that doesn’t mean the industry is back from the brink. BioSpace gathered three early-stage biotech CEOs and members of the NextGen Class of 2026 to talk about surviving and thriving in this rollercoaster...

title: Update: Reversal after 20 deaths allows new Japan patients to take Amgen's rare disease drug Tavneos
snippet: Kissei Pharmaceutical is reversing a recommendation related to Amgen-shared Tavneos that it made just a few days ago, now saying the rare disease drug can be given to new patients.

title: Roche licensing pact clears generic drugmakers to manufacture antiviral flu drug
snippet: By partnering with a UN-backed body, Roche has enabled companies to make the medicine for supply in 129 countries.

title: Lilly’s triple agonist shows bariatric surgery–like weight loss results
snippet: Lilly met analysts’ sky-high expectations with 28.3% weight loss over 80 weeks for the triple hormone receptor agonist retatrutide in a highly anticipated readout on Thursday.

title: Vaxart seeks shareholder support as battle over stock split brews
snippet: In September last year, a group of concerned stockholders raised alarm about Vaxart’s proposed reverse stock split, which the biotech was pushing for despite strong opposition from shareholders.

title: Lilly, BMS, Incyte strike deals to keep biopharma’s AI integration rolling
snippet: A trifecta of newly inked tech partnerships—from Eli Lilly, Bristol Myers Squibb and Incyte—exemplify the increasingly central role that AI is playing in drug development.

title: BioMarin bounces back as Voxzogo performs ‘bigger than expected’ in new indication
snippet: After being hit by safety issues and subpar results in another trial, BioMarin’s Phase 3 test of Voxzogo for a rare skeletal disorder called hypochondroplasia showed efficacy “solidly above” what the drug has shown for achondroplasia, which causes dwarfism.

title: Inside Germany’s biotech capital infrastructure and innovation engine
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, as part of our series on the European life sciences investment ecosystem, you'll be hearing from Regina Hodits, managing director at Angelini Ventures and Sofia Ioannidou, VC partner at Andera Life Sciences. They explore Germany’s biotech and life sciences ecosystem, including the science...

title: Biopharma professionals increasingly accept underemployment
snippet: The number of biotech and pharma professionals taking jobs they’re overqualified for is now over 50%, based on a BioSpace LinkedIn poll. A recruiting firm executive discusses the trend, the reasons behind it and why it doesn’t have to derail careers.

title: Lilly and Engage Bio tie the knot with $202M acquisition
snippet: Eli Lilly joins hands with Engage Bio, acquiring the DNA delivery platform developer in hopes of bolstering its genetic medicines portfolio.

title: FDA sends warning letter after Chinese supplier breaks GLP-1 import restrictions
snippet: While the manufacturer is on the list of authorized GLP-1 importers, FDA inspectors found the company relabeled APIs from another site in a potential attempt to “circumvent safeguards.”

title: Pazdur declines to announce candidacy for vacated FDA commissioner role
snippet: FDA veteran Richard Pazdur outlined his priorities for the next FDA commissioner but stopped short of putting himself forward as a candidate to lead the agency at an RBC Capital Markets event on Tuesday.

title: Moderna’s stock rides hantavirus fears as spotlight returns to infectious disease platform
snippet: While the pathogen appears unlikely to trigger a pandemic, analysts see potential for Moderna to build goodwill amid a period of political pressure on vaccine manufacturers.

title: Beam one-ups Wave as both show promise of genetic editing for AATD
snippet: While both Beam Therapeutics and Wave Life Sciences touted notable biomarker benefits for their respective alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency assets, analysts said that Beam might have the efficacy advantage as Wave’s drug hits an efficacy “ceiling.”

title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

title: Deep learning multi-omics integration identifies new molecular subtypes of lung cancer.
snippet: Cancer is a heterogeneous disease, with numerous subtypes differing in molecular profiles, risk factors, clinical outcomes, and tumor locations. Lung cancer, the third most diagnosed cancer in the United States, is driven by a complex combination of molecular alterations, which influence tumor behavior and patient outcomes. title: Development of a question prompt list to support Consent for Genomic Testing (CoGenT) and research.
snippet: While genomic testing is increasingly utilised to personalise cancer treatment, its complexity creates challenges in achieving informed consent. title: Distinct genomic and immunologic tumor evolution in germline TP53-driven breast cancers.
snippet: Pathogenic germline TP53 alterations cause Li-Fraumeni Syndrome (LFS), and breast cancer (BC) is the most common cancer in LFS females. Here, we perform a multimodal analysis, comparing LFS-BC with sporadic premenopausal breast cancer which shows that nearly all LFS-BC undergo biallelic loss of TP53, with no recurrent oncogenic variants except ERBB2 (HER2)....

title: The Natural Quassinoid Brusatol Enhances Venetoclax Efficacy and Overcomes Resistance in Myeloid Leukemias.
snippet: While venetoclax-based combinations have shown promising results in acute myeloid leukemia (AML), the remission duration is generally short, warranting strategies to further improve efficacy and overcome resistance. title: CSF1R-dependent CD169-positive macrophages locally constrain melanoma growth in the skin.
snippet: Macrophages in the skin reside in multiple distinct layers and perform various functions. Here, we show that CD169+ macrophages reside in the hypodermis and comprise the major skin myeloid cell population in the steady state. In a syngeneic melanoma model, CD169+ macrophages encapsulate growing melanomas and directly suppress their growth. CSF1R blockade de...

title: UniversalEPI: robust prediction of cell type-specific and differential chromatin interactions from DNA sequence and chromatin accessibility.
snippet: Enhancer-promoter interactions (EPIs) play a central role in gene regulation, but experimental techniques such as Hi-C for mapping these interactions remain costly and labor-intensive. title: Cancer: A bioelectric disease?
snippet: Cancer progression is driven by coordinated alterations in signalling networks that regulate proliferation, plasticity, metabolism and therapeutic response. title: Preclinical evaluation of a multi-epitope mRNA vaccine platform for broad and durable SARS-CoV-2 protection.
snippet: The emergence of immune-evasive SARS-CoV-2 variants has exposed limitations in the breadth and durability of protection conferred by current Spike-based vaccines, highlighting the need for next-generation approaches targeting conserved viral regions. title: Integrated characterization of distinct immune subtypes linked to drug responsiveness in breast cancer.
snippet: Precision medicine emphasizes accurate patient stratification. However, existing molecular frameworks often fail to fully capture the intrinsic heterogeneity of breast cancer (BRCA). title: Association of Pain With Mental and Physical Function in Patients With Chemotherapy-Induced Peripheral Neuropathy: A Secondary Analysis From Two Randomized Controlled Trials.
snippet: Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy (CIPN) affects 30%-60% of patients who receive neurotoxic chemotherapy. title: Understanding the dual role of granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor in the lung cancer tumor microenvironment and its therapeutic implications.
snippet: Granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) is a pleiotropic cytokine that plays a complex and critical dual regulatory role in the development, progression, and immunotherapy of lung cancer. title: Identification of prognostic biomarkers and immunotherapy response predictors in lung adenocarcinoma: integrative Mendelian randomization and machine learning analysis.
snippet: Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD), the most prevalent and clinically heterogeneous form of lung cancer, lacks robust biomarkers for prognosis and immunotherapy response prediction. title: Single-cell transcriptome analysis identifies neutrophil-related prognostic signatures in hepatocellular carcinoma.
snippet: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) remains a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, with neutrophils playing complex dual roles in tumor progression and immune regulation. title: Single-nucleus RNA sequencing reveals the effects of key regulators on human pancreatic adenocarcinoma and its tumor microenvironment.
snippet: The aggressive nature and poor prognosis of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) are closely linked to its complex tumor microenvironment (TME). snippet: Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP), a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon from tobacco smoke, exhaust, and pollutants, is linked to bladder cancer (BLCA). title: Expression pattern, functional characteristics and prognostic significance of NLGN1 gene in nasopharyngeal carcinoma: a comprehensive multi-omics analysis.
snippet: Neuroligin 1 (NLGN1), as an important member of the cell adhesion molecule family, primarily participates in synapse formation and neuronal connection regulation. title: TLR7 induces autophagy in non-small cell lung cancer tumor cells and influences anti-tumors responses in patients.
snippet: Autophagy is a fundamental catabolic process with context-dependent roles in cancer. snippet: HIV latency remains a major barrier to achieving a definitive cure, as replication-competent proviruses persist in resting CD4⁺ T cells despite prolonged antiretroviral therapy (ART). snippet: This review provides a comprehensive overview of macrophage heterogeneity, polarisation dynamics, metabolic reprogramming and multicellular interactions of macrophages in psoriasis. title: Spatial Transcriptomics and Bulk RNA-Seq Analysis Revealed Molecular Classification of Invasive Lobular Carcinoma.

title: Spatial multi-omics reveals a cancer-associated fibroblast-T cell niche sustaining tissue-resident immunity in clear-cell renal cell carcinoma.
snippet: Clear-cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) has a lot of immune cells in it, but the performance of the body's immune system against ccRCC and the outcome for patients who have ccRCC vary greatly. title: A Mitoxyperilysis-Related Single-Cell and Machine-Learning Framework Defines an Immune-Cold Melanoma Phenotype and a Robust Prognostic Signature.
snippet: Mitoxyperilysis is a mitochondria-dependent membrane lysis process driven by innate immune and metabolic cues, yet its clinical relevance in melanoma remains unclear. We analyzed single-cell RNA-seq data (GSE215120) to quantify a mitoxyperilysis-related score (MRS), resolve cell-type heterogeneity, and compare predicted cell-cell communication between MRS-h...

title: Two-year treatment-free sustained remission after chemo-immunotherapy in a 52-year-old male with recurrent-mutant lung adenocarcinoma: a case report.
snippet: Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) have reshaped the management of advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), yet only a subset of patients derive durable benefit and reliable predictive biomarkers remain limited. AT-rich interaction domain 1A () alterations have been identified as potential modulators of tumor immunogenicity, although clinical evidence....

title: Understanding the dual role of granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor in the lung cancer tumor microenvironment and its therapeutic implications.
snippet: Granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) is a pleiotropic cytokine that plays a complex and critical dual regulatory role in the development, progression, and immunotherapy of lung cancer. In recent years, with the deepening of research into the tumor microenvironment (TME), GM-CSF has been recognized to possess dual regulatory functions. O...

title: Consolidative thoracic radiotherapy after first-line chemoimmunotherapy in extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer: a multicenter retrospective study.
snippet: While consolidative thoracic radiotherapy (cTRT) is standard after chemotherapy for extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC), its role in the immunotherapy era remains unclear, and this study provides real-world evidence on its survival benefit and safety. This multicenter, retrospective cohort study included patients diagnosed with ES-SCLC who unde...

title: Impact of effective dose to immune cells (EDIC) on survival in limited-stage small cell lung cancer treated with radiotherapy and immunotherapy.
snippet: Concurrent chemoradiotherapy-immunotherapy is promising for limited-stage small cell lung cancer (LS-SCLC), but biomarkers integrating local and systemic immunity are needed. This study evaluates the prognostic value of combining effective dose to immune cells (EDIC) with hematological indicators in these patients. In this retrospective cohort study of 174....

title: Methylated circulating tumor DNA: technical challenges and clinical applications in non-small cell lung cancer patients-a narrative review.
snippet: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) accounting for the majority of cases. Tissue biopsies are invasive and often limited, emphasizing the need for minimally invasive biomarkers. Circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) obtained through liquid biopsy enables non-invasive tumor profiling. While....

title: Identification of prognostic biomarkers and immunotherapy response predictors in lung adenocarcinoma: integrative Mendelian randomization and machine learning analysis.
snippet: Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD), the most prevalent and clinically heterogeneous form of lung cancer, lacks robust biomarkers for prognosis and immunotherapy response prediction. This study aimed to identify key prognostic genes and develop a predictive framework to guide personalized treatment strategies. We integrated summary data-based Mendelian randomization...

title: Air pollution, driver mutations, and survival in non-small cell lung cancer: insights from a high-altitude Hispanic cohort.
snippet: Lung cancer remains a critical global health concern, with a substantial fraction arising in never-smokers. Air pollution-including particulate matter (PM, PM), nitrogen dioxide (NO), sulfur dioxide (SO), and ozone (O)-has been implicated in lung carcinogenesis; however, studies linking these exposures to molecular biomarkers in Hispanic populations remain....

title: From biology to the clinic: research evidence and therapeutic advances of TROP2-targeted antibody-drug conjugates in small cell lung cancer.
snippet: Trophoblast cell surface antigen 2 (TROP2) is a type I transmembrane glycoprotein that was initially identified in studies related to trophoblastic cells and has been implicated in epithelial cell proliferation and the maintenance of cellular stemness. While TROP2 is expressed at low levels in normal epithelial tissues, it is markedly overexpressed in a wid...
